title: Golden Crisp Korean Pomegranate Dosirak
description: A nutty and crisp Korean dish from Gangwon, featuring pomegranate and fermented ingredients, perfect for a cozy meal. This lacto-vegetarian-friendly recipe is served in a dosirak, a traditional Korean lunchbox.

ingredients.name: grated lacto-vegetarian cheese
ingredients.notes: such as mozzarella or Monterey Jack

instructions: Rinse the short grain rice thoroughly and cook it according to the package instructions.
In a large pan, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat and add the Korean chili flakes, cooking for 1 minute.
Add the fermented soybean paste to the pan and cook for an additional 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Stir in the lacto-fermented kimchi and cook for 2 minutes, until heated through.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked rice, kimchi mixture, pomegranate seeds, toasted sesame seeds, and green onions.
Season the mixture with salt to taste.
Transfer the mixture to a dosirak or a heatproof container and top with grated lacto-vegetarian cheese.
Bake the dosirak in a preheated oven at 375 degrees Fahrenheit for 10 minutes, until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the dosirak from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the Golden Crisp Korean Pomegranate Dosirak hot, garnished with additional pomegranate seeds and green onions if desired.
